instrument.pip_value_note: 1 point = 0.01 USD per 0.01 lot; confirm broker tick_size at runtime

ea_identity.name: AntigravityGeoScalper
ea_identity.philosophy: Every entry, exit, stop-loss and take-profit level must be derived exclusively from geometric and mathematical constructs. No lagging indicator is permitted. Price action is treated as a geometric object moving through a two-dimensional Cartesian space where the X-axis is time (bar index) and the Y-axis is price.
ea_identity.constraint: ZERO use of RSI, MACD, MA crossovers, Bollinger Bands, or any convolution-based indicator. Only pure coordinate geometry, ratio algebra, angular mathematics, fractal recursion, and vector calculus are allowed.

timeframes.strategy: Top-down confluence. H4/H1 defines geometric structure. M15/M5 refines harmonic zone. M1 fires entry on candlestick geometry confirmation.

account_module.description: Retrieve and expose all key account metrics at OnInit() and refresh at each OnTick()
fields_required: SymbolInfoInteger(symbol, SYMBOL_SPREAD) — live spread in points

slippage.note: Pass as deviation parameter in OrderSend(). For scalping on XAUUSD, recommended ≤ 30 points.

1_fibonacci_engine.description: All Fibonacci constructs derive from the golden ratio φ = (1 + √5) / 2 ≈ 1.61803398875

swing_detection.algorithm: ZigZag via fractal pivot: a swing high is a bar whose high is the maximum over [i-N, i+N] bars; analogous for swing low. N = input parameter (default 5).
swing_detection.coordinates: Each swing is a Cartesian point P(bar_index, price)

golden_spiral.description: Project a logarithmic spiral from the last significant pivot. Radius grows by φ per 90° rotation. Convert polar (r, θ) to price-time (price, bar_index) using r = A * e^(b*θ) where b = ln(φ) / (π/2).
golden_spiral.arc_price_targets: Where spiral intersects horizontal price levels → candidate reversal zones

price_clusters.description: Run Fibonacci from multiple swing pairs on all active timeframes. Cluster overlapping levels within tolerance band. Cluster density score = number of overlapping levels.

2_harmonic_pattern_engine.description: Harmonic patterns are geometric price formations defined by strict Fibonacci ratio relationships between five XABCD points in Cartesian price-time space.
2_harmonic_pattern_engine.coordinate_system: X(bar, price), A(bar, price), B(bar, price), C(bar, price), D(bar, price)

Shark.note: D lies at 0.886 of XA extension — extreme harmonic

validation.Potential_Reversal_Zone: PRZ = D-point ± cluster of CD projection, BC extension, and XA retracement levels within tolerance
validation.pattern_quality_score: Sum of ratio errors for all legs; lower = higher quality. Only trade score < 0.15.

2_harmonic_pattern_engine.signal: Bullish harmonic → long at D when price enters PRZ from above. Bearish harmonic → short at D when price enters PRZ from below.

3_gann_engine.description: W.D. Gann's geometric theory: time and price are equivalent dimensions. The most important geometric line is the 1×1 (45°) angle from a significant pivot.

gann_angles.formula: Price at bar N from pivot = PivotPrice + (N * GannUnit * tan(angle_rad))
gann_angles.GannUnit: One time unit = one bar. Price unit calibrated so that 45° angle slope = AverageBarRange / 1 bar.

square_of_9.description: Spiral-based price calculator. Numbers spiral outward from center. Cardinal cross and diagonal cross angles (45°, 90°, 135°, 180°, 270°, 360°) mark support/resistance.
square_of_9.formula: Target = (√Price + N * 0.25)² where N is the number of 90° cardinal steps
square_of_9.apply_to: Current XAUUSD price — generate 8 angular targets above and below

square_of_range.description: Square the price range of the current swing. Side of the square in price = Side in time.
square_of_range.formula: range = SwingHigh - SwingLow; time_projection_bars = round(range / PointValue)

gann_fan.pivot_type: Last confirmed swing high or swing low on M15/H1
gann_fan.lines: Draw all 9 Gann angle lines from pivot. Price sitting above 1x1 = bullish. Price sitting below 1x1 = bearish.
gann_fan.break_signal: Price crossing through an angle line = momentum shift; trade direction of the break.

time_cycles.note: These are Fibonacci-number bar counts. Project forward from last significant pivot. Cycle completion bar = candidate reversal.
time_cycles.geometric_time_cluster: When 2+ cycle projections converge within ±3 bars = high-probability time node

4_vector_momentum_engine.description: Model each price leg as a 2D vector V = (Δtime, ΔPrice). Apply vector algebra to measure momentum, compare legs, and project targets.

vector_construction.V_current: Vector from last confirmed pivot to current price
vector_construction.V_previous: Vector of the preceding same-direction leg
vector_construction.angle: θ = atan2(ΔPrice / PointValue, Δbar) in degrees

momentum_signals.angle_comparison: If θ_current > θ_previous → steeper move = strengthening; else weakening
momentum_signals.dot_product: V_current · V_previous; if positive = trend continuation; if negative = reversal pressure

pythagorean_projection.description: Use the Pythagorean theorem in price-time space to project where the current vector leg will terminate.
pythagorean_projection.target_magnitude_source: Equal to 0.618 × or 1.618 × of previous leg magnitude

linear_regression_channel.description: Fit a least-squares line through last N bars. Channel = ±1σ and ±2σ bands.
linear_regression_channel.formula: y = mx + b; m = (n·Σxy − Σx·Σy) / (n·Σx² − (Σx)²); b = (Σy − m·Σx) / n
linear_regression_channel.signal: Price at +2σ with bearish candle geometry → short scalp. Price at −2σ with bullish candle geometry → long scalp.

5_circle_arc_engine.description: Treat significant price pivots as points on a circle. Use circle geometry to project price targets and time targets.
5_circle_arc_engine.radius_formula: r = distance from selected pivot to current price in (bar, price) normalized space
5_circle_arc_engine.arc_projection: Draw arc of radius r centered on pivot. Where the arc intersects future price levels = geometric target.

midpoint_perpendicular.description: Perpendicular bisector of any two pivot points. Price approaching this geometric bisector = balanced/indecision zone.

5_circle_arc_engine.inscribed_angle_theorem: Angle subtended by a chord at the circumference = half of central angle. Use to validate proportion of price moves.

6_fractal_geometry_engine.description: Markets are self-similar (fractal). The same geometric patterns repeat at different scales.

fractal_pivot_detection.rule: A fractal high at bar i: High[i] > High[i-1] AND High[i] > High[i-2] AND High[i] > High[i+1] AND High[i] > High[i+2]. Analogous for fractal low.
fractal_pivot_detection.mql5_function: iBands or custom fractal pivot scanner across all active timeframes

self_similarity_score.description: Compare the ratio (leg2/leg1) on M5 with the ratio on M15 and H1. If all three timeframe ratios are within 5% of each other = fractal alignment.
self_similarity_score.signal_boost: Fractal alignment on 3 TFs multiplies confluence score by 1.5

hausdorff_dimension_proxy.description: Approximate fractal dimension D = log(N) / log(r) where N = number of sub-segments after partitioning a move and r = scaling ratio. D close to 1 = trending; D close to 1.5 = random/choppy.
hausdorff_dimension_proxy.trade_filter: Only trade when D < 1.3 (trending behavior confirmed geometrically)

candlestick_geometry_module.description: Every candlestick is a geometric object with measurable properties. No Japanese interpretation heuristics — only pure ratio and angle mathematics.

definitions.CandleSlope: atan2(Close - Open, 1) in degrees — angle of the candle body
definitions.EngulfArea: BodySize[i] vs BodySize[i-1]; engulf if BodySize[i] > BodySize[i-1] * 1.0

Doji.signal: Geometric indecision — open and close nearly identical; potential reversal pivot

Pin_Bar_Bullish.signal: Price rejected geometrically from low — buy signal at PRZ

Pin_Bar_Bearish.signal: Price rejected geometrically from high — sell signal at PRZ

Bullish_Engulf.condition: Close[i] > Open[i] AND Open[i] < Close[i-1] AND Close[i] > Open[i-1] AND BodyRatio[i] > 0.55
Bullish_Engulf.signal: Geometrically dominant bullish body absorbs prior bearish body

Bearish_Engulf.condition: Close[i] < Open[i] AND Open[i] > Close[i-1] AND Close[i] < Open[i-1] AND BodyRatio[i] > 0.55
Bearish_Engulf.signal: Geometrically dominant bearish body absorbs prior bullish body

Inside_Bar.signal: Price compressing within prior candle's geometric range — breakout imminent

Outside_Bar.signal: Engulfing the prior bar's full geometric range — expansion candle

Marubozu_Bullish.condition: BodyRatio > 0.92 AND UpperWickRatio < 0.04 AND LowerWickRatio < 0.04 AND Close > Open
Marubozu_Bullish.signal: Maximum geometric bullish momentum — almost pure body, minimal wicks

Marubozu_Bearish.condition: BodyRatio > 0.92 AND UpperWickRatio < 0.04 AND LowerWickRatio < 0.04 AND Close < Open

Three_Bar_Reversal.condition: Three consecutive candles where slope alternates: down-down-up (bullish) or up-up-down (bearish). Measured by CandleSlope angle.
Three_Bar_Reversal.signal: Geometric turn in three-candle momentum vector

display_requirements.visual_panel: Draw a dedicated sub-window on the chart showing: BodyRatio, UpperWickRatio, LowerWickRatio as horizontal bar gauges. Display pattern name detected. Color code: green for bullish signal, red for bearish, gray for neutral.
display_requirements.chart_labels: Draw an ObjectCreate arrow or label on the chart at each detected candlestick pattern, named with pattern type and geometric score.
display_requirements.dashboard_panel: Top-left corner HUD showing: current pattern | body ratio | wick ratios | confluence score | PRZ zone level | active signal direction | SL | TP

confluence_scoring_system.description: Aggregate all geometric signals into a single Confluence Score (0–100). Trade only when score ≥ threshold.

Vector_momentum_alignment.rule: Dot product positive + φ-ratio velocity = 15; one = 8

Candlestick_geometry_match.rule: Pin bar or engulf at PRZ = 10; Doji at PRZ = 7; inside bar = 5

confluence_scoring_system.direction_rule: All active geometric signals must agree on direction. Any conflict between Fibonacci and Gann direction = reduce score by 20 pts.

entry.trigger: Confluence score ≥ threshold AND current M1 candle closes with confirming candlestick geometry pattern

stop_loss.rule: SL = nearest Gann angle line beyond entry point, OR the most recent fractal pivot beyond the PRZ, whichever is closer. Minimum SL = 80 points for XAUUSD.

take_profit.TP1: 0.618 × leg magnitude projected from entry — partial close 50%
take_profit.TP3: 1.618 × (φ extension) from entry — final 20% or trail

trailing_stop.method: Geometric trail — move SL to the next Gann angle level behind price as it advances

position_management.break_even_rule: Move SL to entry + 5 points when price moves 40 points in favor
position_management.no_trade_zones: Avoid entries within 15 minutes of major news (implement via NewsFilter flag)

scalper_specific_settings.preferred_session: London open (07:00–10:00 GMT) and New York open (13:00–16:00 GMT) — highest geometric pattern completion rate for XAUUSD
scalper_specific_settings.target_pips_per_trade: 15–40 points scalp (XAUUSD 0.01 point = 1 cent per 0.01 lot)

tick_geometry.description: On M1, analyze last 20 bars for micro-harmonic completion. The same XABCD rules apply at tick-level resolution.
tick_geometry.micro_Fibonacci: Apply 0.382 and 0.618 retracements on M1 swings for precision entry within PRZ

chart_objects: Draw all active Fibonacci retracement levels as horizontal lines with labels
Draw Gann fan lines from last swing pivot on M15 and H1 charts
Shade the PRZ (Potential Reversal Zone) as a rectangle on chart
Draw the linear regression channel with σ1 and σ2 bands
Mark detected harmonic XABCD pattern points with labeled arrows
Mark fractal pivots with triangles (up = swing high, down = swing low)
Draw Gann arc circles from significant pivots
Annotate each detected candlestick pattern with its name and geometric ratios

info_panel_hud.location: Top-left chart corner using ObjectCreate with OBJ_LABEL
fields: SL: XXXX.XX | TP1: XXXX.XX | TP2: XXXX.XX | TP3: XXXX.XX
Last Pattern: [pattern name] | Body Ratio: X.XX | Wick Ratio: X.XX
Fractal TF Alignment: M5 M15 H1

core_functions: OnInit()           — validate symbol, load account data, initialize geometry engines
OnTick()           — gate by new bar, run all geometry engines, compute confluence, execute if threshold met
DetectSwings()     — fractal pivot scanner, all timeframes
RunHarmonicEngine()— XABCD scanner, ratio validator, PRZ builder
RunGannEngine()    — angle lines, Square of 9, fan, time cycles
RunVectorEngine()  — leg vectors, dot product, LR channel
AnalyzeCandleGeometry() — all ratio computations, pattern detection, chart labeling
ComputeConfluence()     — weighted sum, direction consensus check
CalculateLot()          — dynamic lot via risk formula
PlaceOrder()            — OrderSend with SL/TP from geometry
ManagePositions()       — break-even, partial closes, geometric trail
DrawHUD()               — info panel refresh every tick
DrawChartObjects()      — Fibonacci lines, Gann fans, PRZ rectangle, harmonic labels

error_handling.spread_too_wide: If spread > InpMaxSpread, skip entry and log to journal
error_handling.insufficient_margin: Check free margin before order; skip if lot would exceed 80% of free margin
error_handling.pattern_conflict: If bullish and bearish harmonic found simultaneously, do not trade; log conflict
error_handling.geometry_undefined: If fewer than 3 fractal pivots detected on any TF, mark that engine as inactive for this tick

notes.broker_symbol_warning: Symbol may be GOLD, XAUUSD, GOLD.i#, or XAUUSDm depending on broker. Add symbol normalization at OnInit().
notes.decimal_precision: XAUUSD uses 2 decimal places (0.01 point). Always use NormalizeDouble(price, _Digits).
notes.backtest_recommendation: Optimize InpSwingLookback (3–8), InpLRBars (30–80), InpMinConflScore (55–80) via MT5 Strategy Tester on M1 data, 2022–2024.
notes.execution_speed: Scalper EA requires ECN/STP broker with raw spreads < 20 points on XAUUSD during active sessions.
